title: HEATED
description: A newsletter for people who are pissed off about the climate crisis.

items.title: Can PatagoniaTM own Patagonia?
items.description: And more importantly, should it?

items.title: Why Kate Marvel left NASA
items.description: The prominent climate scientist explains how pronouns have become more important than the planet at the nation's space agency.

items.title: Why Shein bought Everlane
items.description: On this week’s podcast episode, Tracy and I go deep about fashion industry greenwashing.

items.title: Everlane was never eco-friendly
items.description: Everlane helped sell the idea that buying lots of new clothes could be good for the climate. Now that marketing belongs to Shein.

items.title: Hantavirus is a climate story
items.description: Scientists tell HEATED the hantavirus outbreak is a warning that climate change is scrambling the boundaries between humans, wildlife, and disease.

items.title: Trump’s NOAA cuts would save less than a day and a half of Iran War spending
items.description: There's always money for war. But when it comes to protecting people from climate disasters, suddenly we're told the cupboard is bare.

items.title: UPDATE: Trump’s DOJ swoops in to save Big Oil
items.description: Trump’s DOJ just sued Minnesota to stop its climate deception case, right as it was finally allowed to move toward discovery.

items.title: Plastic detox update #1
items.description: I expected there’d be a high financial cost to avoiding plastic. I didn’t realize there’d be a social cost, too.

items.title: Republicans introduce extreme bill to ban lawsuits against Big Oil forever
items.description: Shouldn't Democrats... be saying something about this?

items.title: Detoxing my life, resentfully
items.description: Some thoughts on plastic, power, and personal responsibility.

items.title: What should we ask the plastic doctor?
items.description: I'm interviewing her tomorrow!

items.title: Chevron's CEO made $104 million while America bombed Iran
items.description: America's oil executives have pocketed $1.4 billion selling stock during the Iran war, a new investigation shows.

items.title: Oil worker says fracking waste eroded his jaw
items.description: An elementary school was built atop the site where the radioactive waste was spread, journalist Saul Elbein reports.

items.title: Why smart people believe myths about electric cars
items.description: EVs should be having their moment, but misinformation is standing in the way. We spoke to a researcher about how to course correct.

items.title: What oil actually costs
items.description: The market price of oil has never reflected its true cost. This week made that impossible to ignore.

items.title: "Is it a condom or is it gasoline?"
items.description: Tracy and I play a game.

items.title: How fossil fuel ads manipulate us
items.description: On today’s podcast, we watch and analyze fossil fuel ads.

items.title: Fossil fuel propaganda is evolving
items.description: A new analysis of nearly 2,000 fossil fuel ads finds Big Oil has moved from green promises to insisting oil and gas are inevitable. (They're not).

items.title: Our reaction to the Steyer interview
items.description: Just some immediate, off-the-cuff thoughts.

items.title: Can a billionaire fix California?
items.description: Tom Steyer argues he's not a regular billionaire. He's a cool billionaire.
